meta.title: Unit 2 — Common & Proper Nouns (Test 9)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Review test with a focus on locating proper nouns in contexts.

questions.question: Which word is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "America" is a proper noun — it's the name of a specific country.

questions.question: "Home" is a common noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "home" refers to any home — it's a common noun.

questions.question: What type of noun is "December"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: December is a proper noun — months are specific names and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which word is a common noun?
questions.explanation: "home" is a common noun — it refers to any home.

questions.question: "saturday" is correctly written.
questions.explanation: Saturday is a proper noun (day of the week) — must be capitalized: Saturday.

questions.question: What type of noun is "corn"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: corn is a common noun — it refers to any corn, not a specific item.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: home/corn = general words (common). America/December/Sunday = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "This summer, I will take a trip to America," the capitalization is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! "America" is a proper noun (country name) and is correctly capitalized.

questions.question: Which word needs a capital letter: "we buy snacks at familymart."?
questions.explanation: "familymart" should be "FamilyMart" — it's a proper noun (store name). Also, "we" needs a capital at sentence start.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Taiwan"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: Taiwan is a proper noun — it's the specific name of a country.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "This summer I will take a trip to America." America is a proper noun.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: corn/road = general words (common). Taiwan/Dragon Boat Festival/December = specific names (proper).

questions.question: "June" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! June is a month name — months are proper nouns and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence is correctly capitalized?
options: we go to the library on monday.
We go to the library on Monday.
We go to the Library on Monday.
We Go To The Library On Monday.
questions.explanation: "Monday" is a proper noun (day of week) — capitalized. "library" is a common noun — lowercase.

questions.question: Fix this sentence: 'The park is on the busy street.' Which noun is a proper noun if we say 'Main Street'?
questions.hint: A specific street name is a proper noun
questions.explanation: "Main Street" is a proper noun (it's a specific street name) — capitalize both words.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "There are so many people on Main Street." Main Street is a proper noun.

questions.question: Which sentence has all capitalization correct?
questions.explanation: "The baby is sleeping." — only the first word needs a capital. "baby" is a common noun — lowercase.

questions.question: In the sentence "We go to the park on saturday," there is a capitalization error.
questions.explanation: "saturday" should be "Saturday" — it's a day of the week (proper noun) and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: home/road = general words (common). America/Taiwan/June = specific names (proper).

questions.question: Fix this: 'my teacher's name is ms. jones'. Write the teacher's name correctly.
questions.hint: Both the title and surname need capital letters
questions.explanation: "Ms. Jones" is a proper noun (person's name) — both the title (Ms.) and surname (Jones) need capitals.
